text: fat is 100g. cholesterol, saturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for fats, quality matters far more than quantity here.
carbs is 0g. the rest is not doing much. carbs are almost irrelevant in this category.
protein is 0g. everything else is kind of whatever. protein is barely part of the case in this category.
vitamin e at 11% DV, vitamin k at 1% DV, vitamin a at 0% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
iron at 0% DV, magnesium at 0% DV, calcium at 0% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

the upsides first. highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here. efficient energy source because that can be a strength in the right context. helps meals actually work because a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one.

on the weaker side. extremely calorie dense by default because that is the unavoidable category burden. easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. not a complete food on its own because even the better fats are still just part of a meal.
Peanut Oil is A tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
